data.name: Localized Title
data.value:
Lokalizowany tytuł ```
But maybe "Localized Title" could be "Tytuł lokalizacji"? Let's consider typical UI translation for "Localized Title". Usually "Lokalalizacja" is the process. "Localized Title" means "Title (that has been) localized". In Polish that is "Tytuł zlokalizowany". However, "zlokalizowany" is past participle, meaning "has been localized". "Tytuł zlokalizowany" is more precise.
Let's consider morphological nuance: "zlokalizowany" is a past participle from "zlokalizować" (to localize). "Lokalizowany" is present passive participle from "lokalizować" (to localize). "Lokalizowany tytuł" implies "title being localized". "Zlokalizowany tytuł" implies "title that has been localized". Which is more appropriate depends on context: if it's a field to input the localized title, you may want "Tytuł zlokalizowany". But many UI translations use "lokalizowany" for "localized". For instance, "localized resource" could be "zasób lokalizowany". However, "zlokalizowany" is also used.

Actually let's think: If you have a resource file called "LocalizedTitle" maybe it's a label. I think "Lokalizowany tytuł" is more straightforward.

Let's examine the context: The paragraph includes a UI for a localization editor. The "Localized Title" is likely a label for an input field where you enter the localized title for a language. For example, when editing a localized article, you have fields "Title" and "Localized Title". The UI code snippet:
``` <label for="localized-title" class="form-label">@Localizer["Localized Title"]</label> <input type="text" id="localized-title" class="form-control form-control-lg" placeholder="@Localizer["Translated title"]" maxlength="200" /> ```
Thus "Localized Title" is a label. The placeholder for the input is "Translated title". So we need to translate "Localized Title". In Polish UI, you could translate this as "Lokalizowany tytuł". However, "Lokalizowany" is a present participle meaning "being localized". The placeholder "Translated title" maybe "Przetłumaczony tytuł".
